mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation rise in the industrial production index over the next 5 trading days signals a supply-side shock that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 5-day average return in supply-side shock periods (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% decline in the unemployment rate over the next 60 trading days signals a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return is not significantly lower than the 60-day average return in tightening labor market periods (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in the housing market index over the next 20 trading days signals a boost in consumer confidence and spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 20-day average return in consumer confidence boost periods (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 50-basis-point rise in the federal funds rate over the next 5 trading days signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return is not significantly lower than the 5-day average return in monetary tightening cycle periods (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-standard-deviation decline in the industrial production index over the next 60 trading days signals a supply-side shock that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 60-day average return in supply-side shock periods (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% rise in the unemployment rate over the next 20 trading days signals a loosening labor market that boosts wage growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return is not significantly lower than the 20-day average return in loosening labor market periods (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% decline in the real GDP over the next 5 trading days signals a contraction in consumer spending and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return is not significantly lower than the 5-day average return in contraction periods (t-test p < 0.05).
